pi_items.source: NREL 5MW technical report states 'blade length: 60.5 m, attached to a hub with radius 2.5 m' but detailed specs show 61.5m truncated blade span

pi_items.verified_value: Maximum power coefficient Cp = 0.482 to 0.486 at optimal TSR and zero pitch angle

pi_items.verified_value: Blade Element Momentum (BEM) is a real aerodynamic analysis method implemented in QBlade combining 1D actuator disc theory with blade element theory

pi_items.verified_value: Multi-column numeric data containing Cp (power coefficient) and TSR (tip speed ratio) columns

privileged_info_summary: The run_bem_simulation task uses the NREL 5MW Reference Wind Turbine (126m rotor diameter, 61.5m blade length), a well-documented reference design from NREL/TP-500-38060. The task executes a steady-state Blade Element Momentum (BEM) aerodynamic simulation in QBlade v0.96.2 with a tip speed ratio (TSR) sweep. The NREL 5MW rotor has a documented maximum power coefficient of approximately 0.482-0.486 at an optimal TSR of 7.55. The expected output is bem_results.txt containing multi-column numeric data with Cp (power coefficient) and TSR columns, exported to /home/ga/Documents/projects/bem_results.txt. Sample NREL 5MW projects are pre-loaded in the environment at /home/ga/Documents/sample_projects/.
